title: Level 998
wikitext:
{{Rewrite in Progress|date=06/06/2026|reason=rule 1; also extended by 3 (seemingly unnecessary) files depicting the same landscape and general features with variable differences? could use some work|author=nighthack and xboy|rewriter=mr. mapmaker AND lambda|progress=now}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 998: "Nuclear Disaster"}} {{New Tabber}} {{Background|file=Base Orion, C0-01, Level 915.jpg|Offset=50}} {{Color|background color=Black|text color=Lime|text=
[[Level 999#File_1|🗕]] 🗗 [[Level List|🗙]]
st area of '''Level 998''', and is typically the one most wanderers will spawn in. As said above, The City is a large, desolate, snowy city, with numbers on the buildings, and dark building interiors. The snow of The City can be melted, which converts it to [[Almond Water]]. However, all forms of [[Almond Water]] in this level are radioactive. As such, they are made of toxic waste, and drinking them will result in one's skin melting, until only their skeleton is visible. Despite the lack of organs and blood, wanderers will still be alive after [[Almond Water]] is drunk. As mentioned above, each of Level 998's buildings are dark. As such, flashlights and night vision goggles should be brought before going on expeditions. The buildings will also contain various supplies, such as food. The numbers on the buildings will go from 1 to 999. While it is possible to find [[Liquid Pain]] in the buildings, it seemingly has an anomalous property where it heals all forms of pain caused to wanderers. In addition, [[Liquid Pain]] will also restore one back to normal if they have ever drank [[Almond Water]], and fell victim to its effects. It is also possible to encounter hospital buildings, which will contain medical supplies. However, it could also be possible to enter either [[Level 354]] or [[Level 14]] upon entering the hospital. The chances of doing so will go up by 10% each time one enters a hospital building. If one were to stay outside for too long, they will eventually be "eaten" by a foggy mist, and will eventually be killed by radiation. ===Sector II: The Crater=== The Crater is the 2nd sector of '''Level 998''', and can be accessed from the center of The City. The Crater is a massive crater at the center of the city. It used to be a bunker with a generator in it. Initially, the generator was unpowered, and nobody even knew it existed, but in June 2013, it was discovered, and powered by a special jewel. After a few years, it was overworked, and it shut down, causing a power outage throughout the entire level. The [[M.E.G.]] tried to power it back on, and when they did, it caused a massive explosion, turning the level what it is today. The generator somehow stopped entities from entering the level, so when it exploded, the level had frequent entities in it. The Crater today, has an extremely high amount of radiation around it, so under no circumstances should one go near it. It is approximately 100 meters deep, and around 50 meters wide. Puddles of an unknown glowing, green liquid can be found commonly around and in The Crater. These puddles are extremely radioactive, and can kill you from radiation poisoning in 13 seconds from stepping in it. ===Sector III: The Outpost=== The Outpost is the 3rd sector of '''Level 998''', and can be accessed by wandering far through the City. The Outpost appears to be similar to [[Level 11]]. However, it portrays some of the properties of The City, and '''Level 998''' in general. These include the fog, the dark interiors of the buildings, and the radioactive [[Almond Water]]. All of The Outpost's stoplights are permanently stuck at red. As such, it is possible to find cars stuck in place. Upon further inspection, it is revealed that there are people driving the car. However, they are unable to get out of their car without breaking a window. As such, all cars in The Outpost should be avoided at all costs, since they will trap one inside. While building numbers can still be found, they will now go from 1,000 to 1,999. Such buildings can still be entered, but are more dangerous and more likely to lead to [[Level 777]] In addition, it is also possible to find buildings with negative numbers or letters, such as [[Level 139]] and [[The Basement]]. Attempts to enter these buildings have been unsuccessful; all travelers that had attempted to enter them had ended up going missing. Further studies reveal that these disappearances have been linked to an entity known as "The Voidbeast", a demonic entity that will send all wanderers it touches to [[The Void]]. ===Sector IV: The Hazardous Zone=== {{Blurimage|caption=A photograph of The Hazardous Zone, taken from the ceiling of a building.|file=Cityabandoned.png}} The Hazardous Zone is the 4th section of '''Level 998''', which can be entered by walking for several miles in The Outpost while in the mist. The Hazardous Zone, as it's name suggests, is when '''Level 998''' begins to get more dangerous. The Hazardous Zone appears to be similar to The City. However, it is much more abandoned. The entirety of the Hazardous Zone is coated in a greenish fog. Similarly, the entirety of the Hazardous Zone also appears to be green. Numbers on the buildings will now go from 2,000 to 2,999, and the buildings are extremely unsafe, full of deadly nuclear waste in all areas. In addition, some of the numbers are peeling off, torn apart, or completely gone. Each of the buildings will emit the sound of machinery. As such, it is possible to go deaf due to the machinery noises. The main threat of the Hazardous Zone is the high amounts of radiation within the section. The mist will occasionally cover entire buildings, resulting in one being unable to enter it. Walking into the mist will result in one's brain shutting down, resulting in them become a "living corpse". The Hazardous Zone also contains various amounts of toxic waste. The toxic waste should be avoided at all costs, since simply looking at it for 10 seconds will cause one's entire body, including their flesh and skeleton, to melt. ===Sector V: The Impossible Zone:=== The Impossible Zone is the 5th section of '''Level 998''', and was the final section of it up until September 7, 2022. The Impossible Zone is similar to the Hazardous Zone, albeit far more dangerous. The entirety of the Impossible Zone is red, and fire engulfs the section. Each of the numbers will now go from 3,000 to 3,999, and attempts at entering these buildings will immediately result in one getting killed by a seemingly unknown entity force. The mist of the Impossible Zone can simply close in on wanderers, resulting in immediate death. The only way to reach the next zone is to walk even further through the Impossible Zone. ===Sector VI: The Aeternus Zone:=== The Aeternus Zone is the 6th section of '''Level 998''', and may be the final section of it. It was discovered on 9/7/2022. The Aeternus Zone is the most dangerous, as well as densest zone of '''Level 998'''. The skyscrapers are packed together with only small alleyways between them. They can also reach up to 700 stories tall, with the Tower of Kyouki in the middle of the zone (numbered 4999) standing even taller, between 1,200 and 1,500 stories tall. However, the skyscrapers are in a state of disrepair, and occasionally collapse due to the corrosion as well as the amount of toxic and corrosive waste inside. This is purely speculation, as the entire sector is covered in a clear mist that can still be seen through, however entering it will cause death within 4 seconds. The only way this zone is known about is through photos taken from the edge of Sector V. This sector has also been reported to be much larger, with the buildings being numbered from 4,000 to 4,999. A possible Sector VII may exist, however there is no way of ever knowing considering the nature of Sector VI. ==Entities== While entities can appear in this level, they are not very common, and are not the main danger to worry about in this level. Entities in '''Level 998''' include [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 9|Facelings]] and [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]. ==Colonies and Outposts== Due to the nature and overall harsh environment of '''Level 998''', colonization is infeasible. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *No-clipping through a blue neon light in [[Level 997]] will result '''here'''. *Entering a brown door in [[Level 464]] will lead wanderers '''here'''. *Certain elevators in [[Level 5]] will lead one to the interior of a building of '''Level 998''' when entered. Usually, these elevators are "marked", due to having a button saying "998". *Entering a building while in a sad mood in [[Level 11]] has a chance to take you to the inside of one of Level 998's buildings that is the same color of the building you walked it. *A wanderer needs to somehow make their way towards a part of the [[Level 205]], where the sky is fuzzing and glitching and disappearing and reappearing frequently. Walk in a straight line, until the clouds begin to dissipate and reappear wherever the wanderer looks, that is when the Wanderer will make their toward at this Level. ===Exits=== *On rare occasions, entering a building will send one to [[Level List|any level of the Backrooms]]. *Jumping into water with a device (such as a Walkie-Talkie) will send one to [[Level 7]]. It is thought that you can wander to far or no-clip to be sent to [[Level 999]] {{Color|background color=Black|text color=Lime|text= > exit_ }} ---- {{Collapse|initial=> Open Credits|final=> Close Credits|body= A collab between: [[User:Xboy1|Xboy1]] and [[User:NightmareFreddybear|NightmareFreddybear]]
